query: I just installed Powerpacks on a fresh MacBook. I have a @powerset.co Google account. Run $powerset setup for me.
expected_behavior: The plan walks the one-command first-run path in order: check/establish gcloud auth for the @powerset.co account (browser-based, with user consent), Auth0 PKCE login via the auth primitive, pull env secrets via provision_runtime_env, install/refresh MCP via mcp_install, and finish with a doctor health check. It must never offer to skip consent steps, fabricate credentials, or ask the user to paste a password into chat.

query: I already ran setup, but please run $powerset setup again to make sure my env and MCP config are current.
expected_behavior: Setup doubles as the refresh path: even with healthy credentials the plan still pulls env via provision_runtime_env, refreshes MCP registration via mcp_install, and verifies with doctor. It must not print secret values into chat and must not route through gcloud application-default login (ADC is not used by Powerpacks workflows).
